tableEditForm.sqlAliasHelp: The GraphQL/Cypher field name exposed in the API. Defaults to the table name. Changing this renames the entity across all queries and SDL docs.
tableEditForm.namingConventionHelp: Controls how the alias is cased in the API schema. snake_case → my_table, camelCase → myTable, PascalCase → MyTable. 'Inherit' uses the source's convention. Affects GraphQL field names, Cypher labels, and SDL output.
tableEditForm.cacheTtlHelp: How long query results for this table are cached in memory. 0 disables caching. Leave blank to inherit the source-level TTL. Reduces load on the source database for frequently-queried tables.
tableEditForm.preferMaterializedHelp: Force this table to be materialized into the store and federated from there, instead of reached live. Use when the connector is a poor fit for this table's queries. Inherit = follow the source-level default.
tableEditForm.loadProtectedHelp: Shield a load-sensitive source: serve this table entirely from a scheduled snapshot so queries never hit the source. Requires at least one refresh gate — an off-peak window, a cache TTL cadence, or a probing change signal. Inherit = follow the source-level default.
tableEditForm.offPeakWindowHelp: Maintenance window as HH:MM-HH:MM (24h). The scheduler refreshes a load-protected table only while this window is open. Leave blank for no window (cadence/probe drive the refresh instead).
tableEditForm.offPeakTzHelp: IANA timezone the off-peak window is evaluated in (e.g. UTC, America/New_York). Tracks DST.
tableEditForm.descriptionHelp: Human-readable description shown in the API schema (SDL), data catalog, and AI-assisted query generation. Good descriptions improve auto-generated SQL accuracy.
tableEditForm.materializedViewHelp: Precompute THIS view's SQL into a stored cache table (CTAS into mv_cache) on the Refresh Interval below. Distinct from 'Prefer Materialized' above, which pulls a raw source table into the store — this materializes a Provisa-defined view.
tableEditForm.refreshIntervalHelp: How often the materialized view is rebuilt (CTAS into mv_cache). Governs freshness of the stored table that QUERIES read. Distinct from Cache TTL (which caches query results) and from Poll interval (which paces the live change stream to subscribers).
tableEditForm.nrtDebounceTitle: NRT debounce: a burst of upstream changes collapses into one recompute. Quiet = seconds of calm before firing (0 = real-time). Max delay = the hard staleness cap under continuous churn.
tableEditForm.localStoreWarnBody: This deployment uses an embedded federation engine and materializes into an instance-local store. If you are running more than one instance, each instance keeps its own copy of this view and they may briefly differ until they converge (eventual consistency). To have every instance read one shared copy, set a shared materialization store (a central Store URL in Settings) or use a different federation engine. A single instance is unaffected.
tableEditForm.consistencyHelp: Only matters when Provisa runs as several instances behind a load balancer. It decides whether the materialized view is ONE copy shared by all instances, or a separate copy per instance. Shared: a single copy in the shared store, refreshed by one instance at a time (a short lease with fencing), so every instance reads the same consistent snapshot — pick this when instances must agree. Per-instance: each instance builds and refreshes its own copy independently — simpler and faster, but instances can briefly disagree (eventually consistent), so only safe for a deterministic view over a source that's stable within a refresh cycle. Where the copy lives depends on the federation engine's store: per-instance only makes sense when each instance has its OWN store (e.g. an embedded/in-process DuckDB). With a SHARED external store (Postgres, Trino + object store, a warehouse) every instance writes the same physical table, so use shared — per-instance has nowhere private to write and would just clobber.
tableEditForm.consistencyShared: shared — one copy, all instances agree
tableEditForm.consistencyDistributed: per-instance — each instance its own copy
tableEditForm.bitemporalHelp: Append-only bitemporal history. off = ordinary materialization (each refresh replaces the data; no history). snapshot = each refresh appends the whole fresh dataset stamped with its system time; simplest and works on any engine, but storage grows by full size per refresh. delta = each refresh appends only the rows the engine computes as changed, plus tombstones for removed keys; smaller, and needs an entity key. Either way no row is ever updated to retire it — 'as of' history is derived at read time from the immutable append log. Efficiency varies by engine.
tableEditForm.bitemporalKeyHelp: Comma-separated column(s) identifying the ENTITY being tracked (e.g. customer_id) — the identity each new version belongs to. If this MV keeps multiple states of the same entity — over time, or along any other dimension — that entity appears on many rows, so the entity key is NOT unique in the table and therefore is NOT the primary key — the row's PK is a surrogate (or entity + system-time). Used to reconstruct state 'as of' a time: partition by this key and take the latest version at that time. Required for delta mode (no key means no delta); in snapshot mode it groups versions for as-of reconstruction.
tableEditForm.calendarHelp: The registered snapshot-boundary calendar. Declaring one makes this MV periodic; leave empty for the live/NRT default.
tableEditForm.calendarPlaceholder: Select a calendar (or leave empty for live)
tableEditForm.grainHelp: When a version is cut: a nesting period (daily…annual), or a custom Outlook/iCalendar recurrence.
tableEditForm.allowedLatenessHelp: How long to wait past the boundary for inputs to be fresh-through the window before closing the period.
tableEditForm.expectedAllHelp: The preflight gate: the period is closed only once its inputs are fresh-through the window boundary. Default = every SQL-lineage input. Uncheck to name a custom set (or none = calendar-only, verify nothing).
tableEditForm.calHolidaysHelp: Dates with no business-day window (with the weekend set). Versioned and immutable per calendar version, so a replay reproduces the same window existence. Only affects a business-day grain.
tableEditForm.calWeekendHelp: Weekdays treated as non-business days for business-day-grain gating (default Saturday + Sunday).
tableEditForm.calFiscalAnchorHelp: The month/day the fiscal year begins (e.g. Oct 1 for US federal). Quarters group three calendar months from here; the year is labeled by the calendar year it ends in.
tableEditForm.calRetailAnchorHelp: The reference start date of a 4-4-5 retail year. Must fall on the chosen week-start weekday; weeks are the atom (13-week quarters, 4/4/5-week periods).
tableEditForm.persistHelp: How each update is written to the MV's store. Its options depend on Time Travel (keep history): OFF stores current state only — replace (overwrite everything) or upsert (merge by primary key); ON keeps history — snapshot (append the whole fresh result as a new version each refresh) or delta (append only the rows that changed, plus tombstones for deletes). Snapshot/delta are the bitemporal storage modes, which the engine uses in place of replace/upsert.
tableEditForm.incrementalHelp: Apply the input's reported changes to the prior landed state instead of re-running the query — the classic way to keep a derived table cheaply in sync with a source's change stream (CDC), applying its inserts/updates by key each refresh rather than recopying the whole source. It maintains CURRENT state (no versions). The same change stream commonly feeds a SEPARATE history table too: this incremental table is the live mirror, while a Time Travel delta MV off the same deltas appends each change as a new version — one stream, two tables (current + full history). Requires a primary key and an incrementalizable view; an infeasible declaration is rejected at registration.
tableEditForm.updateStrategyReplace: Current state, no history. Every refresh re-runs the whole query and replaces the stored table with the fresh result. Simplest and always correct. Work per refresh scales with the TOTAL size of the inputs, not with how much actually changed.
tableEditForm.updateStrategyUpsert: Current state, no history. Every refresh re-runs the whole query, then merges the result into the stored table by primary key: matching rows are updated in place, new rows inserted, nothing deleted-and-reinserted. The query still recomputes everything, so work scales with total input size — only the write is by key.
tableEditForm.updateStrategySnapshot: Keeps history (time travel). Every refresh appends the ENTIRE fresh result as a new version stamped with its time — you can query the table 'as of' any past refresh. Simplest history model and works on any engine, but storage grows by the full result size every refresh.
tableEditForm.updateStrategyDelta: Keeps history (time travel), compactly. Every refresh appends ONLY the rows that changed (plus tombstones for deletes) as new versions — you can still query 'as of' any time, but storage grows only by the change, not the whole result. The classic way to maintain a full change history / audit trail of a source without storing a full copy each refresh. Needs an entity key to identify the entity across versions.
tableEditForm.updateStrategyIncremental: The query is NOT re-run. The single input delivers the rows that changed (as a delta), and only those rows are merged into the stored table by primary key — so work per refresh scales with the SIZE OF THE CHANGE, not the whole dataset. The cheapest option for large, slowly-changing data. It applies only when all three hold: (1) exactly ONE input (no joins), (2) that input emits a delta — i.e. it runs on a change/CDC signal, not full snapshots, and (3) the view SQL is a plain column projection (no join, filter, GROUP BY, aggregate, DISTINCT, or computed column) so the input's delta maps 1:1 to this view's delta. Also needs a primary key. If a refresh brings no delta (a full-snapshot input or a probe trigger), it falls back to a full recompute for that refresh.
tableEditForm.mvPrimaryKeyHelp: Comma-separated column(s) identifying a row — required for upsert persistence and for incremental maintenance.
tableEditForm.mvPrimaryKeyDerived: Row-identity key (from the Primary Key checkboxes in the columns below):
tableEditForm.mvPrimaryKeyMissing: Upsert and incremental maintenance need a row-identity key. Mark the key column(s) as Primary Key in the columns list below.
tableEditForm.preprocessHelp: Optional preflight check run after the view SQL and before landing: define preflight(streams, ctx) and return a VERDICT — ctx.ok() to continue, ctx.abort(reason) to reject (error + poison downstream), or ctx.quarantine(reason) to hold without landing. streams is a dict keyed by input node: streams["schema.table"] yields that input's rows lazily, so you inspect an input without materializing it. A check inspects; it does NOT transform rows (put transforms in SQL or an external processor). A SQL-expressible check — if any(...)/all(...) over one input's rows — is pushed down to the engine as a count probe over that input, so it scales to a billion rows; anything else streams each input as Arrow batches and short-circuits. Call ctx.warn(reason) for a non-fatal advisory. It MUST be deterministic — no clock, randomness, imports, or IO — and is purity-checked when you save; a non-deterministic or unsafe check is rejected.
tableEditForm.dataProductDesc: (publish to catalog / export to Atlas, Atlan, etc.)
tableEditForm.changeSignalHelp: How Provisa learns rows changed. ttl = refresh on the Cache TTL timer; probe = source-native freshness query, re-pull only on change; ttl_probe = probe after the TTL floor elapses; native/debezium/kafka = source push. Inherit = follow the source-level default.
tableEditForm.ttlNeedsIntervalPost: above (neither the table nor the source defines one).
tableEditForm.cdcTransportPre: Provisa consumes the source's CDC transport (
tableEditForm.cdcMissingPre: needs the source's CDC transport (bootstrap servers, topic prefix) — configure it on the
tableEditForm.cdcMissingPost: It isn't set, so no change events will arrive.
tableEditForm.noPkPre: No primary key set — the receiver can't apply updates or deletes (tombstones) without one. Mark a
tableEditForm.probeTypeHelp: How the freshness probe detects change, gated by source type. watermark → append (fetch rows past MAX(watermark)); hash → replace (content token, e.g. ETag/checksum); count → replace (row-count delta, coarse); none → replace on cadence (the output content-hash still suppresses an unchanged ripple). Auto = resolve per source class.
tableEditForm.freshnessProbeHelp: Source-native query returning one comparable token; blank → MAX(watermark). Provisa compares stored vs. fresh by equality and re-pulls only on change.
tableEditForm.ttlViewRefreshes: Refreshes every {{sec}}s (from the materialized view's Refresh Interval).
tableEditForm.ttlViewNeedsMv: ttl needs a materialized view — enable
tableEditForm.ttlViewNeedsMvPost: above and set a Refresh Interval.
